Add the shrimp to the pan in a single layer. Cook for 2 minutes on one side until the edges turn pink.
Flip the shrimp and cook for another 1-2 minutes until they are opaque and have formed a 'C' shape.
Remove from heat immediately. Stir in the fresh lemon juice and chopped parsley. Toss to coat the shrimp in the garlic butter sauce.
Serve hot with a side of crusty bread to soak up the butter or toss with al dente pasta.

name: Beef Tacos
steps: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the ground beef and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until fully browned (about 5-7 minutes).
Drain the excess fat from the skillet.
Add the taco seasoning and 1/4 cup of water. Simmer for 3-5 minutes until the sauce has thickened and coated the beef.
While the beef simmers, place the taco shells on a baking sheet and warm them in the oven at 150°C for 5 minutes until crisp.
To assemble, place a generous spoonful of seasoned beef in each shell.
Top with shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, and a sprinkle of cheddar cheese.
Serve immediately with a side of salsa and sour cream.

name: Fried Rice
steps: Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a wok or large skillet over high heat.
Add the frozen peas and carrots and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until tender and heated through.
Push the vegetables to the sides of the pan. Pour the beaten egg into the center and scramble until set.
Add the cold rice to the pan. Use a spatula to break up any large clumps of rice.
Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes, tossing the rice with the vegetables and egg until the rice is heated through and starting to slightly toast.
Pour the soy sauce and sesame oil over the rice. Toss well to ensure every grain is evenly seasoned.
Stir in the green onions at the last second and serve hot.

name: Pasta Alfredo
steps: Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a boil. Cook the fettuccine until al dente according to package instructions.
While the pasta is cooking, melt the butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Pour in the heavy cream and bring to a gentle simmer. Let it cook for 3-4 minutes until it starts to thicken slightly.
Reserve 1/4 cup of the pasta water, then drain the fettuccine.
Add the hot pasta and the grated parmesan to the skillet. Toss vigorously until the cheese has melted into a smooth, creamy sauce that coats the pasta.
If the sauce is too thick, add a splash of the reserved pasta water. Season with freshly cracked black pepper and serve immediately.

name: Chicken Quesadilla
steps: Heat a large non-stick skillet over medium heat. Lightly grease with cooking spray or a tiny amount of oil.
Place one tortilla in the pan. Sprinkle half of the cheese evenly over the entire surface.
Add the shredded chicken, diced bell peppers, and sliced green onions over the cheese.
Top with the remaining cheese and the second tortilla.
Cook for 3-4 minutes until the bottom tortilla is golden brown and crisp. Carefully flip the quesadilla using a wide spatula.
Cook the other side for another 3 minutes until the second tortilla is golden and the cheese is completely melted.
Slide onto a cutting board, let it rest for 1 minute, then cut into 6 wedges with a pizza cutter.

name: Chicken and Rice Bowl
steps: Cook the brown rice according to package instructions (usually 30-40 minutes). Fluff and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat oil over high heat. Add the cubed chicken and sear until fully cooked and browned (6-8 minutes). Season with garlic powder.
Steam the broccoli florets until tender-crisp (about 5 minutes).
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce and sesame oil.
Combine the cooked chicken and steamed broccoli in the skillet. Pour the sauce over them and toss to coat.
Divide the rice evenly among 4 meal prep containers. Top each with a portion of the chicken and broccoli mixture.
Seal and refrigerate. These bowls are perfect for reheating in the microwave for a quick, healthy lunch throughout the week.

name: Ground Turkey Bowls
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (400°F). Toss the sweet potato cubes with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and smoked paprika. Roast for 20-25 minutes until tender.
While the potatoes roast, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent.
Add the ground turkey to the skillet and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until fully browned.
Once the turkey is cooked, stir in the baby spinach. The residual heat from the turkey will wilt the spinach in about 1-2 minutes.
Combine the roasted sweet potatoes with the turkey and spinach mixture.
Divide the mixture into 4 meal prep containers. These bowls are high in protein and complex carbs, making them an excellent choice for post-workout recovery or a filling lunch.

name: Salmon with Veggies
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (400°F) and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
Place the salmon fillets on one side of the pan and the broccoli and cherry tomatoes on the other side.
Drizzle everything generously with olive oil. Season the salmon and vegetables with oregano, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Place thin slices of lemon directly on top of the salmon fillets to keep them moist and infuse flavor.
Bake for 15-18 minutes until the salmon is opaque and flakes easily with a fork, and the broccoli is slightly charred at the edges.
Let everything cool slightly before dividing into 4 airtight meal prep containers. Add a side of quinoa or brown rice if a higher calorie count is needed for your macros.

name: Egg Muffins
steps: Preheat the oven to 175°C (350°F) and generously grease a 6-cup muffin tin with butter or non-stick spray.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk the eggs with the milk, salt, and white pepper until completely smooth and slightly frothy.
Divide the chopped spinach, green onions, and shredded cheese evenly among the 6 muffin cups.
Pour the egg mixture over the fillings in each cup, filling them about 3/4 of the way to the top to allow for rising.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until the muffins are set in the center and light golden-brown on the edges.
Allow the muffins to cool in the tin for 5 minutes before using a silicone spatula to pop them out. These can be refrigerated for up to 4 days and reheated in the microwave for 30 seconds.

name: Pasta Meal Prep
steps: Boil a large pot of heavily salted water and cook the pasta until al dente (1-2 minutes less than the package instructions, as it will soften more when reheated).
In a separate large saucepan, combine the marinara sauce and the meatballs. Simmer over medium-low heat for 10 minutes until the meatballs are heated through.
Drain the pasta and toss it directly into the sauce with the meatballs, or keep them separate depending on your preference.
Divide the pasta and 3 meatballs into 4 individual meal prep containers.
Top each portion with a tablespoon of grated parmesan and fresh basil leaves.
Store in the refrigerator. When ready to eat, splash a teaspoon of water over the pasta before microwaving to prevent the sauce from drying out.

name: Stir-Fry Boxes
steps: Toss the beef slices with 1 tablespoon of soy sauce and the cornstarch. Let it sit for 10 minutes (this tenderizes the meat via 'velveting').
Heat a wok or large skillet over high heat with 1 tablespoon of oil. Stir-fry the beef in batches until browned. Remove and set aside.
Add the mixed vegetables and ginger to the hot pan. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Add the beef back to the pan along with the remaining soy sauce and sesame oil. Toss for 1 minute until the sauce thickens and coats everything.
Divide the stir-fry among 4 containers. For a complete meal, serve over 1/2 cup of pre-cooked white or brown rice in each box.
Garnish with sesame seeds if available and cool before sealing.

name: Mac and Cheese
steps: Boil the macaroni in salted water for 2 minutes less than the package instructions. Drain and set aside.
In a medium saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Whisk in the flour and cook for 2 minutes to create a blonde roux.
Slowly pour in the milk while whisking constantly. Continue to cook until the sauce thickens (Bechamel).
Remove from heat and stir in 1.5 cups of the cheddar cheese and a pinch of nutmeg until smooth (Mornay sauce).
Combine the cooked macaroni with the cheese sauce and pour into a small baking dish.
Top with the remaining cheese and breadcrumbs. Bake at 190°C (375°F) for 15-20 minutes until the top is golden-brown and the cheese is bubbling.

name: Fried Chicken
steps: Whisk the buttermilk with lemon zest and a pinch of salt in a bowl. Add the chicken drumsticks and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
In a separate shallow dish, mix the flour with oregano, thyme, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Remove a drumstick from the buttermilk, letting excess drip off, then dredge thoroughly in the flour mixture. Press the flour into the skin for a better crunch.
Heat 2 inches of oil in a heavy-bottomed pot to 175°C (350°F).
Fry the chicken in batches for 12-15 minutes, turning occasionally, until the crust is deep golden and the internal temperature reaches 75°C.
Drain on a wire rack and serve with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice.

name: Mashed Potatoes
steps: Cut the peeled potatoes into uniform 2cm chunks. Boil in a pot of salted water with the whole garlic clove until completely fork-tender (about 15-20 minutes).
Drain the potatoes and discard the garlic. Return the potatoes to the hot pot for 1 minute to cook off any remaining surface moisture.
While hot, pass the potatoes through a potato ricer or food mill for the smoothest possible texture.
Gently stir in the cold butter, one tablespoon at a time, until fully incorporated.
Slowly add the warm cream while stirring with a wooden spoon or whisk until the potatoes are light, airy, and silky.
Season with sea salt and serve immediately as a base for stews or roast meats.

name: Chicken Pot Pie
steps: Preheat the oven to 200°C (400°F). Line a 9-inch pie dish with one sheet of puff pastry.
In a bowl, combine the shredded chicken, vegetables, gravy, and oregano. Season with salt and pepper.
Pour the mixture into the prepared pastry shell.
Top with the second puff pastry sheet. Trim the edges and crimp with a fork to seal.
Cut three small slits in the top of the pastry to allow steam to escape during baking.
Brush the top generously with egg wash for a golden finish. Bake for 30-40 minutes until the pastry is puffed and deep golden brown.

name: Beef Stew
steps: In a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, sear the beef cubes in oil over high heat until browned on all sides. Remove and set aside.
Add the carrots and onions (if available) to the pot. Sauté for 5 minutes. Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.
Deglaze the pan with red wine or a splash of broth, scraping up all the brown bits from the bottom.
Add the beef back into the pot along with the broth, potatoes, and thyme.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to very low. Cover and simmer gently for at least 90 minutes until the beef is fork-tender and the sauce has thickened.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. Serve in deep bowls with crusty bread.
equipment: Large Dutch Oven or Heavy Pot

name: Grilled Cheese
steps: Spread a very thin layer of Dijon mustard on the inside of each bread slice.
Place the cheese slices between the bread. Spread the outside of both slices generously with room-temperature butter.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat. Place the sandwich in the pan.
Cook slowly for 3-4 minutes per side. Use a heavy weight or a smaller pan to press down on the sandwich (this helps the cheese melt and the bread brown evenly).
Once both sides are deep golden-brown and the cheese is oozing out the sides, remove from the pan.
Let it sit for 1 minute before slicing diagonally. The slow cooking ensures the bread doesn't burn before the high-quality cheese melts completely.
equipment: Heavy weight or small pan (for pressing)

name: Avocado Toast
steps: Toast the sourdough bread until golden and sturdy enough to hold the toppings.
In a small bowl, mash the avocado with lemon juice, salt, and black pepper until it reaches your desired consistency (chunky or smooth).
Bring a small pot of water to a gentle simmer. Add a teaspoon of vinegar. Create a whirlpool with a spoon and gently drop the egg into the center. Poach for 3 minutes for a runny yolk.
Spread the mashed avocado generously over the toasted bread.
Place the poached egg on top. Garnish with radish slices, red pepper flakes, and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil if desired.

name: Oatmeal Bowl
steps: In a small saucepan, bring the milk to a gentle boil over medium heat.
Stir in the oats and reduce the heat to low. Simmer for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oats are creamy and the liquid is absorbed.
Stir in the cinnamon and chia seeds. If the oatmeal is too thick, add a splash of extra milk.
Transfer the oatmeal to a serving bowl.
Top with fresh berries and a generous drizzle of honey.

name: Smoothie Bowl
steps: Place the frozen berries, frozen banana, and almond milk into a high-speed blender.
Blend on high until the mixture is thick and smooth, similar to soft-serve ice cream. Use a tamper or stop to scrape the sides if necessary.
Pour the thick smoothie into a wide, shallow bowl.
Arrange the granola, hemp seeds, and some fresh fruit (if available) in neat rows on top of the smoothie.
Serve immediately before it melts.

name: Egg Scramble
steps: In a small bowl, whisk the eggs with a pinch of salt and pepper until well combined.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. Add the cherry tomatoes and sauté for 1 minute.
Add the spinach to the pan and toss until just wilted.
Pour the whisked eggs into the pan. Use a spatula to gently push the eggs from the edges to the center, creating soft curds.
Just before the eggs are fully set, stir in the crumbled feta cheese. Remove from heat and serve warm.

name: Greek Yogurt Bowl
steps: Scoop the Greek yogurt into a chilled bowl.
Sprinkle the chopped toasted walnuts and pomegranate seeds evenly over the top.
Drizzle the honey in a spiral motion across the surface.
Finish with a light dusting of ground cinnamon for aroma and serving.

name: Protein Pancakes
steps: In a medium bowl, mash the ripe banana with a fork until almost smooth.
Add the egg, protein powder, ground oats, and baking powder. Mix until a thick batter forms.
Heat a non-stick griddle or pan over medium heat and lightly grease with cooking spray.
Pour 2 tablespoons of batter per pancake. Cook for 2-3 minutes until small bubbles form on the surface.
Flip carefully and cook the other side for 1-2 minutes until golden brown. Serve with fresh fruit.

name: Chicken Wrap
steps: Lay the tortilla flat on a clean surface.
Spread the hummus evenly across the center of the tortilla.
Layer the shredded lettuce, cucumber pieces, and sliced grilled chicken on top of the hummus.
Fold the sides of the tortilla inward, then roll tightly from the bottom to create the wrap.
Optional: Place the wrap in a dry skillet over medium heat for 1 minute per side to toast the exterior.

name: Turkey Sandwich
steps: Spread Dijon mustard on one side of each bread slice.
Layer the Swiss cheese and the turkey slices on one piece of bread.
Top with the tomato slices and a handful of fresh baby spinach.
Close the sandwich with the second bread slice.
Press down gently and cut diagonally for serving.

name: Caesar Salad
steps: Wash and thoroughly dry the romaine lettuce. Chop into bite-sized pieces and place in a large salad bowl.
Add the Caesar dressing and half of the parmesan cheese. Toss well to ensure every leaf is coated.
Top the salad with the sliced grilled chicken and crunchy croutons.
Finish with the remaining parmesan shavings and a crack of fresh black pepper.

name: Rice Bowl
steps: In a small pot, warm the pre-cooked rice and rinsed kidney beans over low heat with a splash of water until steaming.
Transfer the rice and bean mixture to a medium bowl.
Top with the fresh salsa and sliced avocado.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and a squeeze of lime juice for brightness. Mix well before eating.

name: Tuna Salad
steps: Drain the canned tuna thoroughly to prevent the salad from becoming watery.
In a small mixing bowl, flake the tuna with a fork.
Add the mayonnaise, finely diced celery, minced red onion, and lemon juice. The celery and onion provide the essential 'Mediterranean' crunch.
Mix well until the ingredients are evenly distributed. Season with a pinch of black pepper.
Serve the tuna salad in a small bowl with the whole grain crackers on the side for scooping.

name: Vegan Stir-Fry
steps: Press the tofu between paper towels for 10 minutes to remove excess moisture. This is the secret to getting it crispy.
Heat a wok or large skillet over high heat with 1 tablespoon of neutral oil.
Add the tofu cubes and fry for 5-8 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden and crispy on all sides. Remove and set aside.
Add the broccoli, ginger, and garlic to the pan. Stir-fry for 3 minutes until the broccoli is bright green.
Return the tofu to the pan. Pour the soy sauce and sesame oil over the mixture.
Toss for 1 minute until everything is well-coated and steaming hot. Serve immediately.

name: Lentil Soup
steps: Rinse the dry lentils under cold water and check for any small stones.
In a large pot, sauté the onions, carrots, and garlic in a little olive oil until softened (about 5 minutes).
Stir in the cumin and the dry lentils.
Add the vegetable broth and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, partially covered, for 30-35 minutes until the lentils are completely tender.
Taste and season with salt and pepper. For a thicker soup, partially blend with an immersion blender before serving.

name: Chickpea Salad
steps: In a small bowl, whisk together the tahini, lemon juice, and a splash of water until you have a smooth, pourable dressing.
Place the rinsed chickpeas and diced cucumber in a large salad bowl.
Pour the tahini dressing over the vegetables.
Add the chopped parsley and toss gently to ensure every chickpea is coated.
Serve chilled as a light lunch or a side dish.

name: Vegan Tacos
steps: In a small skillet, heat the cooked lentils with the taco seasoning and 2 tablespoons of water over medium heat until fragrant and steaming.
Warm the taco shells in a 150°C oven for 5 minutes until crispy.
Fill each taco shell with a generous portion of the spiced lentils.
Top with shredded cabbage for crunch.
Finish with a dollop of fresh guacamole on top of each taco and serve immediately.

name: Buddha Bowl
steps: Ensure your quinoa and roasted vegetables (sweet potatoes and chickpeas) are prepared and warm.
Place the kale in a bowl with a drop of olive oil and a pinch of salt. 'Massage' the leaves with your hands for 1 minute until they soften and darken.
In a wide bowl, place the cooked quinoa as a base.
Arrange the massaged kale, roasted chickpeas, and sweet potatoes in separate sections on top of the quinoa.
Drizzle the entire bowl with tahini dressing and serve at room temperature.

name: Vegan Curry
steps: In a medium pot, combine the cubed sweet potato and half a cup of water. Simmer until the potato is just tender (about 10 minutes).
Stir in the chickpeas, coconut milk, and curry powder.
Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded.
Stir in the fresh spinach at the last minute; it will wilt in the residual heat.
Serve hot over brown rice or with vegan naan bread.

name: Grilled Chicken Salad
steps: Grill the chicken breast over medium-high heat until fully cooked. Let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing into thin strips.
In a large bowl, toss the mixed greens and cucumber with olive oil and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Place the sliced chicken on top of the dressed greens.
Sprinkle with crumbled feta cheese and a crack of fresh black pepper.
Serve immediately as a clean, low-carb meal.

name: Zucchini Noodles
steps: Use a spiralizer to turn the zucchinis into long, noodle-like strands (Zoodles).
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the minced garlic and sauté for 1 minute.
Add the zucchini noodles to the pan. Sauté for only 2-3 minutes. IMPORTANT: Do not overcook or they will release too much water and become mushy.
Transfer the zucchini noodles to a plate and top with the warm marinara sauce.
Finish with a sprinkle of grated parmesan cheese and fresh basil if available.

name: Lettuce Wrap Burgers
steps: Grill the beef patty over medium-high heat until it reaches your desired level of doneness. Let it rest for 2 minutes.
Wash and thoroughly dry two large lettuce leaves. These will act as your 'bun'.
Place one lettuce leaf on a plate. Layer the tomato, onion, and avocado on top.
Place the cooked beef patty in the center.
Cover with the second lettuce leaf. Secure with a toothpick if necessary and serve with a side of pickles.

name: Salmon and Veggies
steps: Preheat your oven to 200°C (400°F).
Place the salmon and the trimmed asparagus onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle everything with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Place lemon slices directly on the salmon to keep it moist while baking.
Bake for 15-20 minutes until the salmon flakes easily with a fork and the asparagus is tender.

name: Cauliflower Rice Bowl
steps: In a large skillet, brown the ground beef with the diced peppers over medium-high heat until no pink remains.
Stir in the taco seasoning and a splash of water. Simmer for 2 minutes.
Add the cauliflower rice directly to the pan with the beef.
Sauté for 3-5 minutes until the cauliflower rice is heated through and has absorbed the spices.
Transfer to a bowl and top with fresh salsa and a dollop of sour cream if desired.

name: Grilled Salmon
steps: Whisk together the soy sauce, mirin, and grated ginger. Brush this marinade over the salmon fillets.
Heat a grill pan over medium-high heat. Lightly grease with oil.
Grill the salmon for 4-5 minutes per side until beautifully charred and just cooked through.
Steam the asparagus in a separate pan while the salmon grills.
Serve the salmon alongside the asparagus, garnished with fresh lemon slices and a sprinkle of toasted sesame seeds.

name: Cheese Omelet
steps: In a small bowl, whisk the eggs with a pinch of salt until no white streaks remain.
Melt the butter in a 10-inch non-stick pan over medium heat until foamy.
Pour in the eggs. Use a spatula to gently move the cooked edges toward the center, allowing the raw egg to flow to the bottom.
When the top is almost set but still slightly wet, sprinkle the cheese over one half.
Fold the omelet in half and cook for 1 more minute to melt the cheese. Slide onto a plate and garnish with minced chives.

name: Chicken Alfredo Pasta
steps: Boil the penne pasta in a large pot of salted water until al dente. Drain and set aside.
In a medium saucepan, heat the Alfredo sauce over medium-low heat until it begins to simmer.
Add the sliced grilled chicken and the cooked pasta into the saucepan with the sauce.
Toss thoroughly to ensure every piece of pasta is coated in the creamy sauce.
Transfer to a large bowl and garnish with fresh parsley and a sprinkle of parmesan if desired.

name: Tacos
steps: Heat a heavy skillet or cast-iron pan over high heat. Add a tiny amount of oil.
Sear the diced steak for 5-7 minutes until deeply browned and charred on the edges. Remove and let rest for 2 minutes.
Warm the corn tortillas directly over a gas flame for 10 seconds per side, or in a dry pan until soft.
Place two tortillas together for each taco (street style). Add a generous portion of steak to the center.
Top with plenty of raw white onion and fresh cilantro. Serve with lime wedges to be squeezed over just before eating.
